audit.title: Frontend Best Practices & Developer Experience Audit
audit.scope: Comprehensive DX and frontend best practices compliance
audit.executive_summary: Exceptional engineering discipline with enterprise-grade tooling, but critical gaps in onboarding documentation and missing standard repository files create high friction for new contributors.

critical_issues.title: Missing Standard Repository Files
critical_issues.impact: Legal risk, community contribution barrier, security vulnerability reporting gap
details: No LICENSE file (legal requirement for distribution)
No CONTRIBUTING.md (blocks external contributions)
No SECURITY.md (no vulnerability reporting process - healthcare compliance risk)
No CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (community governance gap)
critical_issues.recommendation: Create LICENSE (MIT or Apache 2.0), CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md

critical_issues.title: Broken Onboarding Documentation
critical_issues.impact: New developers cannot self-serve setup, blocks team scaling
details: ONBOARDING.md references non-existent remote script URLs
Scripts exist locally but not accessible via GitHub raw URLs
.nvmrc contains only '20' instead of specific version like '20.18.0'
critical_issues.recommendation: Update ONBOARDING.md to use local script paths as primary method, update .nvmrc to exact version

critical_issues.title: Missing Changeset Configuration
critical_issues.impact: Cannot properly version and release library
details: README.md and CLAUDE.md mention 'Use changesets for version bumps'
No .changeset/ directory exists
No changeset dependency in package.json
No documented release workflow
critical_issues.recommendation: Install @changesets/cli, initialize changesets, document release workflow in CONTRIBUTING.md

high_priority_issues.title: Incomplete Component Development Workflow
high_priority_issues.impact: Component developers lack clear step-by-step guide
details: No single document for 'I want to add a new component'
CLAUDE.md workflow is for agents, not humans
build-plan/05-component-building-guide.md is 2,238 lines (too long)
Unclear when to run CEM generation, how to verify exports
high_priority_issues.recommendation: Create COMPONENT_DEVELOPMENT.md with 5-minute quick start and step-by-step workflow

high_priority_issues.title: Monorepo Navigation Complexity
high_priority_issues.impact: Developer confusion about where to work and which commands to run
details: 5 packages with unclear relationships
Developers don't know when to use root vs package commands
No explanation of Turborepo's role
ONBOARDING.md shows mix of root and package commands with no context
high_priority_issues.recommendation: Add Monorepo Architecture section to README.md with table of packages and when to work in each

high_priority_issues.title: Test Infrastructure Documentation Gap
high_priority_issues.impact: Developers don't understand test requirements or how to debug failures
details: No guide on running tests for single component
No documentation on coverage threshold (80%+?)
No instructions for Vitest UI
No guide on running accessibility tests manually
high_priority_issues.recommendation: Add Testing Guide section to component development docs with commands and coverage requirements

medium_priority_issues.title: Port Configuration Inconsistency
medium_priority_issues.impact: Documentation confusion about dev server URLs
details: ONBOARDING.md says docs run on http://localhost:4321
CLAUDE.md says Storybook on port 6006 (wrong, should be 3151)
CLAUDE.md says Admin on port 3100 (wrong, should be 3159)
medium_priority_issues.recommendation: Standardize port documentation: Docs=4321, Storybook=3151, Admin=3159

medium_priority_issues.title: Missing Error Recovery Documentation
medium_priority_issues.impact: Developers get stuck on common errors
details: No troubleshooting for 'vitest: command not found'
No troubleshooting for 'Module not found: @wc-2026/tokens'
kill-ports.sh script exists but not documented in troubleshooting
medium_priority_issues.recommendation: Expand troubleshooting guide with common errors and solutions

medium_priority_issues.title: VS Code Extensions Not Auto-Installed
medium_priority_issues.impact: Manual setup required, documentation is misleading
details: .vscode/extensions.json exists with recommendations
ONBOARDING.md says 'installed automatically' but this is misleading
VS Code only shows notification to install, not automatic
medium_priority_issues.recommendation: Update ONBOARDING.md to accurately describe VS Code extension prompt behavior

highlights: Source maps and declaration maps enabled
Bundle size budget enforcement (5KB per component, 50KB full bundle)
5,062 lines of test code across 13+ components
Lit externalized (consumers provide their own copy)
Minimal bundle sizes (1.43KB gzipped for full index)
kill-ports.sh script for dev server management

items: Create CONTRIBUTING.md with PR process and component checklist
Create SECURITY.md with vulnerability disclosure process
Add changeset configuration for version management
Add Monorepo Architecture section to README.md
Standardize port documentation across all files
Document test workflow (how to run, debug, check coverage)
Expand troubleshooting guide with common errors
Add test coverage badge to README.md
Document when to run root vs package commands

conclusion.summary: The wc-2026 platform is technically excellent with world-class TypeScript configuration, testing infrastructure, and CI/CD pipelines. However, the developer experience for new contributors is significantly hampered by missing standard repository files, broken onboarding documentation, and lack of clear component development workflow.
conclusion.key_insight: The technical foundation is exceptional. The documentation just needs to catch up.
conclusion.action_required: Invest 12-16 hours over the next week to address CRITICAL and HIGH priority items. This will transform the platform from 'excellent engineering' to 'excellent developer experience' and unlock the potential for community contributions.
